meaning: n.浜洪亾涓讳箟,鍗氱埍涓讳箟
examples: This was a historic leap that even surpassed the humanitarianism of ancient Greece, which was a pioneering idea at the time.
Unlike Shakespeare's most plays that are full of humanitarianism, The Tempest is seriously flawed in this respect.
At some level, certainly in the minds of the medics themselves, this is humanitarianism.
The Bible has affected the world for centuries in innumerable ways, including art, literature, philosophy, government, philanthropy, education, social justice and humanitarianism.
